summary: Full backend + frontend testing for KING NEET AIR. Backend 21/21 pytest tests pass (root, levels, questions, auth, username, quiz submit with XP/chapter/daily bonus, profile, period leaderboards). Frontend: landing renders correctly, hero CTA logic verified via code review (DOM intercept blocked by browser), ProtectedRoute redirects correctly, authenticated dashboard renders user data + KPIs + all mode/subject buttons + navbar-xp, username edit works, full PYQ play flow Dashboard→Play→ModeSelect→Quiz→Result works end-to-end (6 biology questions submitted, result page shows XP and review), Leaderboard tabs (daily/weekly/monthly/all_time) all render and switch correctly with empty state for period tabs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is approaching the 700-line guideline (553 lines) and packs auth, questions, quiz, leaderboard, profile, and level logic in one file. As more features (AI gen, profiles, social) are added, split into modules: routers/auth.py, routers/quiz.py, routers/leaderboard.py, services/levels.py, services/xp.py.

Username uniqueness check during initial signup uses a tight while loop with separate Mongo queries — fine for low volume; consider a unique index on users.username for correctness under race conditions.

No rate-limiting on /api/quiz/submit; a client could spam submits of the same correct answers for the chapter-completion bonus across different chapters/sessions. Consider per-user/per-question debouncing or attempt validation against an issued question batch.

ai_generate_questions only top-ups when mode != 'pyq' AND subject specified AND a subject is set; for mode='mock_test' with subject=None it never tops up, so mock test may return very few questions from the 35-seed corpus.

Mock test (mode='mock_test') has no special handling vs 'pyq' filter — server returns is_pyq=True only for mode='pyq'; for 'mock_test' it returns ANY questions including PYQ and AI, which is fine but worth documenting.

Frontend Landing.jsx hero login handler is correct (window.location.origin + '/dashboard' is passed via encoded redirect). However, automated capture of window.location.href set is blocked by browser security; manual code review confirms correctness.
Daily leaderboard period uses 'last 24h' rather than 'today UTC'. Combined with daily_challenges_completed using today's UTC date, behaviour is consistent but may surprise users in non-UTC timezones.
test_credentials: Seeded test user via mongosh per /app/auth_testing.md. FE session token: fe_tok_1781246425801_hiushkkc (user_id=test-fe-1781246425801, username originally fetestuser1781246425801, later renamed via UI test to fe_edited_246504). Backend tests create/teardown ephemeral users per fixture.
seed_data_creation: Backend startup auto-seeds 35 NEET questions (Bio/Physics/Chem) into db.questions. Test suite seeds TEST_-prefixed users + sessions per test then cleans up. One persistent FE test user remains: test-fe-1781246425801.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py as regression suite (pytest --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml). conftest.py exposes seeded_user, auth_client, seed_user_factory fixtures that handle mongosh seed + cleanup. For FE auth-gated screens, set cookie session_token (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None) on neet-xp-rank.preview.emergentagent.com — the dashboard hydrates from /api/auth/me. Note testids: Dashboard uses mode-pyq-btn / mode-daily_quiz-btn / mode-mock_test-btn while /play uses play-mode-{id}. Quiz route is /quiz?mode=..&subject=..&limit=.. and Result reads sessionStorage.lastResult.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Quiz submit XP rules verified: +10/correct, +50 per chapter completed (only if all chapter questions in batch correct AND chapter not previously completed), +100 daily_quiz when correct >= half of answers AND not already awarded today. Second daily attempt same day correctly returns only the base XP (20 in test, no bonus repeat).
